mainEntity.name: How much does an AJ133 (5.0 V8) engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned AJ133 V8 typically costs £5,500–£9,000 supply only, varying by specification (NA vs SC) and rebuild quality. Used units can be found from £3,000–£5,500, but provenance is critical. Fitting adds £900–£2,000 depending on labour rates.

mainEntity.name: Is the AJ133 (5.0 V8) a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ133 has a mixed reputation (3.5★). Pre‑2013 timing chain tensioner failure is a known issue, and coolant system leaks are common. Post‑2013 revisions improved durability significantly, making later examples far more dependable.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common AJ133 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pre‑2013 timing chain tensioner wear is the most critical — it can cause chain stretch and catastrophic interference. Coolant leaks from crossover pipes and water pump failure are also frequent. Supercharger coupler wear (on SC versions) is another common issue.

mainEntity.name: Which Land Rover models use the AJ133?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ133 powers the Range Rover (L405) with both naturally aspirated and supercharged versions, and the Range Rover Sport (L494) with the supercharged 5.0 V8.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover AJ133 the same as Jaguar's version?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the AJ133 is shared with Jaguar fitments in the XF, XJ, XK, and F‑Type. The engine is mechanically identical, though ECU calibrations and ancillaries differ by model.

hero.h1: Land Rover AJ133 (5.0 V8) Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The AJ133 is Land Rover's flagship 5.0‑litre V8 — a sonorous, powerful unit, but pre‑2013 timing chain tensioners are the main risk. Backed by 620+ real UK enquiries in 2025 [EM-VERIFIED].

rows.value: 2 (Range Rover L405, Range Rover Sport L494)
Also fitted to Jaguar (XF, XJ, F‑Type, XK)

verdictRating.confidence: 86% confidence — based on 620+ real UK enquiries, specialist rebuild reports, and JLR TSB documentation.

rows.score: 8 (Midpoint of Reconditioned (Supply Only) range: £5,500–£9,000 → £7,250 midpoint → falls in £7,000–£9,999 bracket → 8)
12 (Mixed cheap/expensive failures; V8 complexity adds labour, but many repairs are well‑understood)
20 (Very well‑supported via JLR and aftermarket; shared with Jaguar ensures excellent availability)
4 (Reconditioned cost (£7,250 midpoint) as % of lowest typical vehicle value (Range Rover L405 ~£6,000 conservative) = 121% → exceeds 75% → 4)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The AJ133 is a glorious V8 with a legendary soundtrack — but early chain tensioners can fail and coolant leaks are common — buy post‑2013 if possible, or ensure tensioner history.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Also powers the Jaguar XF, XJ, XK, and F‑Type → [Jaguar AJ133 page].

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an AJ133 V8 typically takes 14–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [EM-QUOTE].
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Parts cost is identical across Land Rover and Jaguar applications; labour time varies by model and engine bay access.
